SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.capacity

Class BottleneckTracker

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.capacity.BottleneckTracker
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class BottleneckTracker extends Object implements Serializable
Records how the binding capacity bottleneck of a process changes over time.

A single BottleneckResult answers what limits the plant right now. During a field-life depletion, a dynamic transient, or any rate/allocation sweep the limiting constraint typically migrates — for example from a topside separator early in life to a subsurface well-drawdown limit late in life. BottleneckTracker captures that temporal dimension as a first-class object: the caller records a BottleneckResult at each step (year, second, iteration, ...) and the tracker surfaces the migration events, the peak loading, and a JSON timeline that an agent can hand off downstream.

The tracker deliberately depends only on BottleneckResult (not on ProcessModel or ProcessSystem) so it stays in the leaf capacity package with no circular dependency. Typical usage: 

 BottleneckTracker tracker = new BottleneckTracker();
 for (int year = 0; year <= nYears; year++) {
   plant.run();
   tracker.record(year, "Year " + year, plant.findBottleneck());
   reservoir.runTransient(secondsPerYear);
 }
 for (BottleneckTracker.Snapshot ev : tracker.getMigrationEvents()) {
   System.out.println(ev);
 }
 String json = tracker.toJson();
Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim Development Team

  • Constructor Details

    • BottleneckTracker

      public BottleneckTracker()

  • Method Details

    • record

      public BottleneckTracker.Snapshot record(double time, BottleneckResult result)
      Records the binding bottleneck at a time step.
      Parameters:
      time - the time stamp (caller-defined units)
      result - the bottleneck result to record; an empty result is recorded as an unconstrained snapshot
      Returns:
      the snapshot that was created and stored

    • record

      public BottleneckTracker.Snapshot record(double time, String label, BottleneckResult result)
      Records the binding bottleneck at a labelled time step.
      Parameters:
      time - the time stamp (caller-defined units)
      label - a human-readable label for the step (may be null)
      result - the bottleneck result to record; if null or empty an unconstrained snapshot is recorded
      Returns:
      the snapshot that was created and stored

    • getSnapshots

      public List<BottleneckTracker.Snapshot> getSnapshots()
      Gets all recorded snapshots in chronological order.
      Returns:
      an unmodifiable-style copy of the recorded snapshots

    • getLatest

      public BottleneckTracker.Snapshot getLatest()
      Gets the most recently recorded snapshot.
      Returns:
      the latest snapshot, or null if nothing has been recorded

    • size

      public int size()
      Gets the number of recorded snapshots.
      Returns:
      the snapshot count

    • isEmpty

      public boolean isEmpty()
      Checks whether any snapshots have been recorded.
      Returns:
      true if no snapshots have been recorded

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all recorded snapshots.

    • getMigrationEvents

      public List<BottleneckTracker.Snapshot> getMigrationEvents()
      Returns the snapshots at which the binding bottleneck changed identity.

      The first recorded snapshot is always treated as a migration event (the initial bottleneck). Thereafter a snapshot is a migration event when its "equipment::constraint" identity differs from the previous snapshot. This pinpoints exactly when — and to what — the limiting constraint handed over, e.g. topside separator to subsurface well drawdown.

      Returns:
      the list of migration-event snapshots in chronological order

    • getMigrationCount

      public int getMigrationCount()
      Gets the number of times the binding bottleneck changed identity after the first snapshot.
      Returns:
      the number of migration transitions (0 if the bottleneck never changed)

    • getDistinctBottleneckEquipment

      public List<String> getDistinctBottleneckEquipment()
      Gets the distinct bottleneck equipment names in first-appearance order.
      Returns:
      the list of distinct equipment that was ever the bottleneck

    • getPeakSnapshot

      public BottleneckTracker.Snapshot getPeakSnapshot()
      Gets the snapshot with the highest binding-constraint utilization.
      Returns:
      the peak-utilization snapshot, or null if nothing has been recorded

    • getPeakUtilizationPercent

      public double getPeakUtilizationPercent()
      Gets the highest binding-constraint utilization recorded.
      Returns:
      the peak utilization in percent, or 0.0 if nothing has been recorded

    • getTimelineSummary

      public String getTimelineSummary()
      Builds a human-readable timeline of the bottleneck migration.
      Returns:
      a multi-line summary listing each migration event

    • toJson

      public String toJson()
      Serializes the recorded timeline to a schema-versioned JSON string.

      The JSON contains the full snapshot list, the migration-event list, the peak utilization and the distinct bottleneck equipment, so an agent can reason about the temporal capacity behaviour without re-running the study.

      Returns:
      a JSON document describing the bottleneck timeline

    • appendSnapshotArray

      private void appendSnapshotArray(StringBuilder sb, List<BottleneckTracker.Snapshot> items)
      Appends a JSON array of snapshots to the supplied builder.
      Parameters:
      sb - the builder to append to
      items - the snapshots to serialize

    • formatNumber

      private static String formatNumber(double value)
      Formats a double for JSON output, emitting null for non-finite values.
      Parameters:
      value - the value to format
      Returns:
      the JSON number literal, or "null" if the value is not finite

    • escape

      private static String escape(String value)
      Escapes a string for safe inclusion in a JSON document.
      Parameters:
      value - the string to escape (may be null)
      Returns:
      the escaped string, never null
